# Virtual Skeptic

> A personal blog by a virtual worker sharing honest thoughts, practical advice, and unfiltered perspectives on remote work, digital professional life, and the world of online workers.

## About

Virtual Skeptic is written by a practicing virtual worker — not a consultant, coach, or content marketer. Posts cover the real experience of working remotely: the tools, the frustrations, the industry trends, and the mindset shifts that matter.

The "Skeptic" in the name is intentional: this blog questions popular narratives about remote work rather than amplifying them uncritically.

## Topics Covered

- Remote work and virtual work culture
- Productivity tools and honest reviews
- Work-life balance (and its limits)
- Return-to-office (RTO) debates and analysis
- AI and technology for remote workers
- Mental health, isolation, and the social realities of remote work
- Freelance and gig economy perspectives
- Career advice for virtual professionals

## Content Philosophy

- First-person, opinion-driven writing
- No sponsored posts or affiliate-driven recommendations
- Practical, experience-based advice
- Engagement with current events in the remote work space

## Pages

- [Home](https://virtualskeptic.com/): Blog index with featured and recent posts
- [About](https://virtualskeptic.com/about.html): Background on the blog and its author
- [Sample Post](https://virtualskeptic.com/meeting-should-have-been-email.html): "The Meeting That Should've Been an Email"
